children.heading: Department of Transportation Authority to condition assistance on continuation of service
children.label: b Department of Transportation Authority to condition assistance on continuation of service

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation is authorized to require, to the extent reasonable and practicable, an air carrier provided financial assistance under this part to maintain scheduled air transportation service, as the Secretary of Transportation deems necessary, to ensure services to any point served by that carrier before .March 1, 2020

children.heading: Required considerations
children.label: 2 Required considerations
children.text: When considering whether to exercise the authority provided by this section, the Secretary of Transportation shall take into consideration the air transportation needs of small and remote communities and the need to maintain well-functioning health care supply chains, including medical devices and supplies, and pharmaceutical supply chains.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: 3 Sunset
children.text: The authority provided under this subsection shall terminate on , and any requirements issued by the Secretary of Transportation under this subsection shall cease to apply after that date.March 1, 2022

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If, after , a contractor expends any funds made available pursuant to and distributed pursuant to , the assurances in paragraphs (1) through (3) of subsection (a) shall continue to apply until the dates included in such paragraphs, or the date on which the contractor fully expends such financial assistance, whichever is later.December 27, 2020section 9072 of this titlesection 9073 of this title

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 2 Special rule
children.text: Not later than , each contractor described in that has received funds pursuant to such shall report to the Secretary on the amount of such funds that the contractor has expended through . If the contractor has expended an amount that is less than 100 percent of the total amount of funds the contractor received under such section, the Secretary shall initiate an action to recover any funds that remain unexpended as of .April 5, 2021March 31, 2021April 30, 2021section 9071(3)(A)(i) of this titlesection 9072 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), any contractor that has unspent financial assistance provided under this part as of , and conducted involuntary furloughs or reduced pay rates and benefits, between , and the date on which the contractor entered into an agreement with the Secretary related to financial assistance under this part, shall recall (as defined in ) employees who were involuntarily furloughed during such period by not later than .December 27, 2020March 27, 2020January 4, 2021section 9071 of this title

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall waive the requirement under paragraph (1) for a contractor to recall employees if the contractor certifies that the contractor has or will have insufficient remaining financial assistance provided under this part to keep recalled employees employed for more than two weeks upon returning to work.

children.heading: Audits
children.label: 3 Audits
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury shall audit certifications made under paragraph (2).
